commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(cron): skip stored delivery context for delivery.mode=none sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Cron jobs with `delivery.mode=none` explicitly opt out of auto-announce delivery
- When resolving delivery targets, the stored delivery context from `none`-mode cron sessions was still being used as a fallback delivery vehicle, bypassing the `delivery.mode=none` guard
- This caused duplicate messages arriving via alternating channels — the intended session and an unrelated `none`-mode cron session

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Cron / Delivery

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts` → 54/54 passed (including new regression test)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` → 51/51 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/delivery.test.ts` → 18/18 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/delivery-context.test.ts src/cron/delivery-plan.test.ts src/cron/delivery-preview.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts` → 77/77 passed

## Impact Assessment

- **Scope**: `resolveDeliveryTarget` in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.ts` — the central cron delivery target resolver
- **Downstream**: The new `deliveryMode` parameter is optional (backward-compatible); existing callers that omit it preserve current behavior. Callers updated: `run.ts` (main delivery path), `delivery.ts` (resolveCronAnnounceDelivery)
- **Edge cases**: When `deliveryMode` is undefined or not "none", behavior is unchanged. When "none", stored delivery context is skipped entirely
- **Hard-coded values**: None introduced

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Skip stored delivery context resolution when `delivery.mode=none` so sessions that opted out of auto-announce are never selected as delivery vehicles.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, vitest

**Steps run after the patch:**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  54 passed (54)
```

**Observed result:** All 54 tests pass including the new regression test "skips stored deliveryContext when delivery.mode=none so none-mode sessions are never used as fallback delivery vehicles (#94164)". The test verifies that `extractDeliveryInfo` is never called when `deliveryMode: "none"` is set.

**Not tested:** Live cron fleet deployment with multiple concurrent `delivery.mode=none` sessions and a failing primary delivery path.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveDeliveryTarget` used `resolveCronStoredDeliveryContext` unconditionally, which extracts routable delivery context from a session key without checking whether the owning cron job has `delivery.mode=none`
- Missing guardrail: No check existed between the stored delivery context extraction and the delivery.mode contract

## Regression Test Plan

- Test: `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts` — `skips stored deliveryContext when delivery.mode=none...`
- Scenario: Stored delivery context is available but `deliveryMode: "none"` is passed → verify `extractDeliveryInfo` is not called and result falls back to session store entry

Closes #94164
target.author: lilan0125
